content.claim: Hungary's school network is polarizing by settlement type (2021-2024): urban/suburban zones face rising child-teacher ratios (up to 24:1), while by 2024 more than 1,200 of 3,155 settlements had no public educational institutions at all. National primary school sites decreased from 3,609 in 2021 to 3,521 by 2024, shrinking the feeder network from which 5th/7th-grade selection candidates must be drawn.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Data is aggregated across all primary school types; disaggregation for schools capable of feeding 6-/8-year tracks is not provided
The causal link between primary school closure and 6-/8-year track feasibility is inferred rather than directly measured
Overcapacity in urban areas (24:1 ratios) may reflect mixed school types, not specifically gimnazium pressure
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
